Root.title: Toronto 3-Day Trip Plan
Root.pace: Balanced pace; mix of landmarks and neighborhoods

assumptions.assumption: Staying in or near downtown Toronto
Using TTC (subway/streetcars) and walking for most trips
Typical sightseeing days with morning/afternoon/evening blocks
Weather can vary; include indoor backups

Stop.name: CN Tower
Stop.why_go: Signature skyline views and a classic first-time Toronto landmark

Stop.name: Ripley's Aquarium of Canada
Stop.why_go: Easy add-on next to CN Tower; great indoor option

Stop.name: Waterfront walk
Stop.why_go: Relaxed lakeside stroll and city views

Stop.name: St. Lawrence Market
Stop.why_go: One of the city's best food stops; good for browsing and snacks

Transit_Note.from_to: St. Lawrence Market to Distillery District

Stop.name: Distillery District
Stop.why_go: Pedestrian-friendly heritage architecture, shops, and cafes

suggestions.suggestion: Pick a spot along King West for lively nightlife energy
Choose Queen West for a slightly more laid-back, eclectic vibe

Option.name: King West evening
Option.when_to_choose: If you want a busier scene and lots of bars nearby
Option.notes: Great for people-watching; expect crowds on weekends

Option.name: Queen West evening
Option.when_to_choose: If you prefer trendy shops and a more neighborhood feel
Option.notes: Easy to combine with a casual stroll and dessert stops

Option.name: Royal Ontario Museum (ROM)
Option.when_to_choose: If you want a broad mix of natural history, world cultures, and big exhibits
Option.notes: Plan extra time if you like long museum visits

Option.name: Art Gallery of Ontario (AGO)
Option.when_to_choose: If you prefer art-focused collections and a calmer museum pace
Option.notes: Works well before an afternoon of neighborhood wandering

Stop.name: Kensington Market
Stop.why_go: Lively, eclectic streets with shops, murals, and casual eats

Stop.name: Chinatown stroll
Stop.why_go: Fun add-on for browsing and snacks close to Kensington

Option.name: Sunset at Riverdale Park
Option.when_to_choose: If you want an easy, classic skyline view at golden hour
Option.notes: Pairs well with a relaxed evening; bring layers if it is breezy

Option.name: Drinks on Ossington
Option.when_to_choose: If you want a bar-hopping corridor with lots of options
Option.notes: Busy at night; consider going earlier for an easier table

Day.theme: Water and a different side of the city

Transit_Note.from_to: Downtown to Toronto Islands (via ferry terminal)

Stop.name: Toronto Islands
Stop.why_go: Bike/walk routes and some of the best skyline viewpoints

Option.name: Harbourfront
Option.when_to_choose: If you want to stay close to downtown after the Islands
Option.notes: Good for a casual stroll and lakeside breaks

Option.name: High Park
Option.when_to_choose: If you want more greenery and a classic large-city park
Option.notes: Best if you are up for a TTC ride and a slower afternoon

Time_Block.label: Evening
Time_Block.focus: Final dinner in a neighborhood strip

suggestions.suggestion: Little Italy for a lively strip with lots of casual choices
The Danforth for classic Greek options and a neighborhood feel

Option.name: Little Italy dinner
Option.when_to_choose: If you want a vibrant, walkable evening area with many restaurants
Option.notes: Easy to extend with dessert or a short post-dinner stroll

Option.name: The Danforth (Greektown) dinner
Option.when_to_choose: If you want a change of scenery from the core and a food-focused night
Option.notes: Best reached by subway; good for a relaxed final night

Tip.details: TTC subway and streetcars are typically the easiest; tap-to-pay works for most riders.
Consider booking CN Tower and Toronto Islands ferry times on busy days to reduce waiting.
If it rains, swap the Islands/parks for museums like ROM or AGO and keep outdoor walks shorter.
